narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD strength because hot PPI and Fed hawkishness are sustaining dollar demand even as D1 CSM fades, anchoring DXY at 98.50 with upside bias intact
2. AUD outperformance because D1 CSM at 100 with Strong Buy on AUDJPY across both timeframes signals the cleanest directional conviction in the session
3. GBP H4 acceleration because UK data surprise is driving a sharp intraday CSM surge from 0 to 100, creating divergence trades against weaker EUR and CAD

PRICE CONFIRMS

AUDJPY leading the JPY-cross complex is confirmed by its 0.88 correlation with GBPJPY and 0.87 with NZDJPY, meaning any continuation in AUDJPY pulls the entire JPY-cross basket higher. USDCAD Strong Buy on H4 is reinforced by CAD sitting at CSM 2 on D1 and USDCAD holding a -0.61 inverse correlation to AUDUSD, which is itself only modestly bid, keeping the USDCAD setup structurally clean. EURUSD H4 Sell is corroborated by EUR CSM at 38 on H4 and the -0.87 EURUSD-USDCHF correlation pulling CHF strength into the frame as dollar demand persists.

REGIME

Mixed leaning Risk-On confirmed. VIX at 17.81 is falling, S&P 500 is up 0.58% and pushing toward 7444, but Gold at 4709 continuing to rise alongside equities signals residual safe-haven hedging that prevents a clean risk-on declaration.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. USD fundamental vs CSM: News demands USD bullish but D1 CSM for USD has faded to 19 from 76, suggesting the dollar rally is losing structural momentum beneath the surface
2. AUD vs Copper divergence: AUD CSM is the strongest currency at 100 on D1 yet Copper is down 0.35%, undermining the commodity-currency fundamental justification for AUD strength

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: AUDJPY up - D1 and H4 both Strong Buy with cross-JPY correlation confirmation - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: USDCAD up - H4 Strong Buy, CAD CSM near zero, USD fundamental support intact - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: NZDUSD - H4 CSM surge to 99 conflicts with weak D1 CSM at 40 creating whipsaw risk
Watch: A break above the AUDJPY H4 resistance with CADJPY confirming in tandem validates the primary trade and the broader JPY-weakness thesis

themes.theme: USD strength from hot PPI and Fed hawkishness
Trump-Xi meeting providing mixed signals on trade
GBP outperformance on strong UK data
EUR under pressure amid yuan competition and hawkish ECB limited impact
JPY weakness as dollar rally extends
INR weakness from foreign outflows despite tariff support
Commodity-linked currencies consolidating ahead of China data
Fed policy divergence with potential successor Warsh signaling tighter stance

key_observation: USD rallying on hot PPI data and hawkish Fed narrative despite Trump-Xi constructive dialogue. GBP strength from UK economic data outperformance. EUR and JPY under structural pressure. Market pricing out dovish Fed expectations.
watch: Trump-Xi meeting outcomes on trade and tariffs. Further Fed policy guidance. UK BoE June rate decision signals. China economic data response to trade tensions.
